# Gcore CDN

- [CDN](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn.md)

## Getting Started

## Create a CDN resource
- [CDN resource overview](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/create-a-cdn-resource.md): Create CDN resources for entire sites, static assets, or video streaming to accelerate content delivery via Gcore edge servers and caching infrastructure.
- [Create a CDN resource for static assets](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/create-a-cdn-resource/create-a-cdn-resource-for-only-static-files.md): Configure CDN resource with custom domain, origin group, and CNAME record to accelerate static asset delivery.
- [Create a CDN resource for the entire site](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/create-a-cdn-resource/create-a-cdn-resource-for-the-entire-site.md): Create a CDN resource for entire site acceleration using Gcore CDN and Managed DNS to deliver static and dynamic content without code changes.
- [Create a CDN resource for video streaming](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/create-a-cdn-resource/create-a-cdn-resource-for-video-streaming.md): Quick guide to set up Video CDN for delivering HLS, DASH, or FLV live streams and VOD content

## Integrate CDN with CMS and other platforms
- [About Gcore CDN integration with CMS and other platforms](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms.md): Integrate Gcore CDN with CMS platforms (WordPress, Drupal, Magento, Joomla) and cloud storage (AWS S3, Google Cloud Storage) by replacing application domain ...
- [Integrate CDN resource with AWS S3](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-aws-s3.md): Configure Gcore CDN resource with AWS S3 bucket as origin, enable static website hosting, set bucket policy, and verify content delivery through CDN CNAME.
- [Integrate CDN resource with B2evolution](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-b2evolution.md): Configure B2evolution CMS to serve static assets through Gcore CDN by replacing base URL variables with CNAME in configuration file.
- [Integrate CDN resource with Cloudflare](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-clouldflare.md): Configure Gcore CDN CNAME records with Cloudflare DNS by disabling proxying and set Origin Pull Protocol to HTTP or add SNI Hostname to avoid 502 errors.
- [Integrate CDN resource with Concrete5](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-concrete5.md): Install Concrete5 CDN extensions and configure Gcore CDN resource CNAME to rewrite static file URLs through content delivery network.
- [Integrate CDN resource with Craft](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-craft.md): Configure Craft CMS asset sources with Gcore CDN CNAME to serve static files through CDN resource.
- [Integrate CDN resource with Cs-cart](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-cs-cart.md): Install Gcore CDN plugin for Cs-cart, configure CNAME in Universal CDN Add-on settings, and verify static asset URLs are rewritten to CDN domain.
- [Integrate CDN resource with Django CMS](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-django-cms.md): Configure Django CMS urls.py and settings.py to deliver static files via Gcore CDN using CNAME, then run collectstatic command.
- [Integrate CDN resource with Drupal](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-drupal.md): Install Gcore CDN Module in Drupal, configure Origin Pull mode with CNAME, enable Far Future expiration and HTTPS/SSL support.
- [Integrate CDN resource with Google Cloud Storage](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-google-cloud-storage.md): Configure Google Cloud Storage bucket as CDN origin, create CDN resource in Gcore portal, and verify CNAME record setup for content delivery.
- [Integrate CDN resource with IPB](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-ipb.md): Configure IPB CMS to deliver static assets through Gcore CDN by setting CNAME in System settings image, CSS, and JS URL fields.
- [Integrate CDN resource with IPS](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-ips.md): Configure IPS Community CDN integration by entering CNAME in System Settings General Configuration CDN Settings section.
- [Integrate CDN resource with Joomla](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-joomla.md): Install Gcore CDN plugin for Joomla 3.4.1+, configure CNAME in System - CDN plugin settings, verify static asset URLs rewritten to CDN domain.
- [Integrate CDN resource with Kirby](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-kirby.md): Configure Kirby CMS to use Gcore CDN by manually updating CSS/JS paths in header.php and using GetImages plugin for image delivery.
- [Integrate CDN resource with Magento](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-magento.md): Configure Magento Admin Panel System settings to integrate Gcore CDN resource using CNAME, merge JavaScript/CSS files, and set Base Skin URL for HTTP/HTTPS d...
- [Integrate CDN resource with Perch](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-perch.md): Configure Perch CMS to deliver static assets through Gcore CDN by updating configuration files with CDN resource CNAME and bucket paths.
- [Integrate CDN resource with PrestaShop](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-prestashop.md): Configure Gcore CDN CNAME in PrestaShop Advanced Parameters/Performance CCC settings and verify static asset URL rewriting via browser DevTools.
- [Integrate CDN resource with PyroCMS](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-pyrocms.md): Configure Gcore CDN CNAME integration in PyroCMS admin panel Settings to rewrite static asset URLs.
- [Integrate CDN resource with SocialEngine](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-socialengine.md): Configure Gcore CDN CNAME in SocialEngine admin panel Settings/General Settings and verify URL rewriting in browser Network tab.
- [Integrate CDN resource with Typo3](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-typo3.md): Install smile_cdn extension in Typo3 and configure Gcore CDN CNAME to rewrite asset URLs for content delivery.
- [Integrate CDN resource with vBulletin](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-vbulletin.md): Configure vBulletin Replacement Variables to rewrite static asset URLs to Gcore CDN CNAME for content delivery integration.
- [Integrate CDN resource with Webasyst](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-webasyst.md): Configure Gcore CDN CNAME in Webasyst administration settings and verify URL rewriting in browser Network tab.

## Wordpress
- [Integrate CDN resource with WordPress (Gcore plugin)](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/wordpress/integrate-cdn-resource-with-wordpress-gcore-plugin.md): Install and configure Gcore CDN plugin for WordPress to enable CDN delivery with file type, folder path, and exception URL settings.
- [Integrate CDN resource with WordPress (Rocket plugin)](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/wordpress/integrate-cdn-resource-with-wordpress-rocket-plugin.md): Configure Gcore CDN with WordPress using WP Rocket plugin by setting CNAME and hostname replacement for static file delivery.
- [Integrate CDN resource with WordPress (W3 Total Cache plugin)](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/wordpress/integrate-cdn-resource-with-wordpress-w3-total-cache-plugin.md): Configure Gcore CDN with WordPress W3 Total Cache plugin by enabling Generic Mirror CDN type and entering CNAME hostname in Performance settings.
- [Integrate CDN resource with WordPress (WP SuperCache plugin)](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/wordpress/integrate-cdn-resource-with-wordpress-wp-supercache-plugin.md): Configure WP Super Cache plugin CDN tab with Gcore CNAME, enable CDN support, and verify static file URL rewriting in WordPress.
- [Integrate CDN resource with X-Cart](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-x-cart.md): Configure X-Cart 4.4.x+ to deliver static content via Gcore CDN by modifying smarty.php, func.files.php, and template files with CNAME records.
- [Integrate CDN resource with XenForo](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-cms/integrate-cdn-resource-with-xenforo.md): Configure XenForo CDN integration by editing config.php and updating Style image path to Gcore CNAME for static asset delivery.
- [Extend CDN functionality with FastEdge](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/integrate-cdn-with-fastedge.md): Integrate FastEdge serverless compute with CDN edge locations to extend content delivery with custom logic and request/response manipulation.

## Configure an origin
- [Add CDN servers to the origin ACL whitelist](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/configure-an-origin/add-cdn-servers-to-the-origin-acl-whitelist.md): Whitelist CDN edge servers on origin via HTTP headers, IP addresses, or subnets using API endpoints or request headers.
- [Сonfigure HTTP/2 on the origin](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/configure-an-origin/configure-http-2-on-the-origin.md): Enable HTTP/2 protocol on origin servers using Nginx 1.9.5+ or Apache 2.4.17+ with mod_http2 module for improved CDN content delivery performance.
- [CDN timeouts](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/getting-started/cdn-timeouts.md): Configure proxy_read_timeout, proxy_connect_timeout, proxy_send_timeout, and client_header_timeout directives for Gcore Edge Network CDN origin servers.
- [About CDN resources interface: how it is arranged](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/about-cdn-resources-interface-how-it-is-arranged.md): Navigate CDN resources interface: create resources, filter by status (Active, Suspended, In processing, Deleted), search by CNAME or ID, and access Settings,...
- [Add an origin group to a CDN resource](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/add-an-origin-group.md): Create and attach origin groups with single or multiple origins to CDN resources, configuring failover logic and round-robin distribution between primary and...

## CDN resource options

## General
- [Specify an origin and the origin pull protocol](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/general/specify-an-origin-and-the-origin-pull-protocol.md): Configure origin server address and select HTTP, HTTPS, or HTTP/HTTPS protocol for CDN content retrieval from origin servers.
- [Use a private bucket as an origin](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/general/use-a-private-bucket-as-an-origin.md): Configure private S3 buckets as CDN origins using AWS Signature V4 authentication with Access Key ID, Secret Access Key, Hostname, and Region credentials.
- [Enable origin SSL validation](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/general/enable-origin-ssl-validation.md): Configure CDN origin SSL validation by uploading X.509 certificates via API and enabling proxy_ssl_enabled, proxy_ssl_ca, proxy_ssl_data parameters for certi...
- [Create and set a custom domain for the content delivery via CDN](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/general/create-and-set-a-custom-domain-for-the-content-delivery-via-cdn.md): Configure custom domains and CNAME records for CDN static content delivery; set up DNS aliases pointing to Gcore's gcdn.co endpoint.
- [Enable and configure origin shielding](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/general/enable-and-configure-origin-shielding.md): Enable origin shielding in Gcore CDN Resource settings to deploy a precache server between origin and CDN edge servers, reducing origin load during traffic b...
- [Suspend a CDN resource automatically or manually](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/general/suspend-a-cdn-resource-automatically-or-manually.md): Suspend CDN resources automatically after 90 days of inactivity or manually via Content availability toggle in resource settings.
- [Customize connection and read timeouts](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/general/customize-connection-and-read-timeouts.md): Configure custom connection and read timeouts for CDN resources to control edge node wait duration when connecting to origin servers and receiving responses.

## Cache
- [Specify cache lifetime on a CDN resource or origin](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/cache/specify-cache-lifetime-on-a-cdn-resource-or-origin.md): Configure Cache-Control header max-age and CDN caching lifetime via origin server directives or CDN control panel to control file retention duration.
- [Set up a CDN resource to follow origin redirects](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/cache/set-up-a-cdn-resource-to-follow-origin-redirects.md): Enable CDN to follow origin HTTP 3xx redirects (301, 302, 303, 307, 308) via Portal or API with configurable status codes.
- [Specify cache lifetime for user browsers](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/cache/specify-cache-lifetime-for-user-browsers.md): Configure browser cache lifetime using Origin-controlled Cache-Control headers or CDN-controlled max-age values via Gcore Customer Portal.
- [Ignore the set cookie or query string parameters when caching content on CDN servers](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/cache/ignore-the-set-cookie-or-query-string-parameters-when-caching-content-on-cdn-servers.md): Configure CDN cache behavior for query string parameters using Ignore All, Ignore All Except, or Ignore Only modes to control cache key generation.
- [Always online: provide the stale cache if the origin is unavailable](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/cache/always-online-provide-the-stale-cache-if-the-origin-is-unavailable.md): Serve stale cached content when origin is unavailable due to network errors, timeouts, or HTTP 5xx/4xx errors; configure error codes in Resource Advanced Set...
- [Сache Sharding: share cache between CDN servers](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/cache/cache-sharding-share-cache-between-cdn-servers.md): Enable Cache Sharding to distribute cached content across CDN servers within a PoP using consistent hashing, reducing origin requests and improving cache hit...
- [Modify cache key](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/cache/modify-cache-key.md): Configure cache key variables (URI, protocol, normalized URI) in CDN resource settings to control cache object identification and improve hit ratio.

## Access
- [Control access to the content with country, referrer, IP, and user agents policies](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/security/control-access-to-the-content-with-country-referrer-ip-and-user-agents-policies.md): Configure CDN resource access restrictions using country, referrer, IP address, and user agent policies via Portal or API.
- [Set up a redirect from HTTP to HTTPS](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/security/set-up-a-redirect-from-http-to-https.md): Enable HTTP to HTTPS redirect on CDN resources via API or Portal after configuring SSL certificates in General and Access sections.

## Secure token
- [About secure token](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/security/use-a-secure-token/about-secure-token.md): Protect CDN content from unauthorized access using Secure Token with IP allowlisting and URL expiration to restrict downloads by time or source IP address.
- [Configure and use secure token](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/security/use-a-secure-token/configure-and-use-secure-token.md): Enable secure token authentication with MD5 hash validation, IP allowlisting, and path-based access control for CDN content delivery.
- [Specify HTTP methods allowed for content requests from the CDN](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/security/specify-http-methods-allowed-for-content-requests-from-the-cdn.md): Configure allowed HTTP methods (GET, HEAD, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, OPTIONS) for CDN resources to block unsupported requests and return 405/501 responses.
- [Set the hostname passed in SNI requests to the origin](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/security/set-the-hostname-passed-in-sni-requests-to-the-origin-server.md): Configure SNI hostname in HTTPS origin requests using dynamic or custom hostname modes in CDN resource security settings.
- [Specify TLS versions allowed for content requests from the CDN](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/security/choose-tls-versions.md): Configure allowed TLS protocol versions (SSLv3, TLSv1, TLSv1.1, TLSv1.2, TLSv1.3) for CDN content requests via Gcore Customer Portal.

## Security
- [Protect CDN resources with Gcore WAAP](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/cdn-security/protect-cdn-resources-with-basic-waf.md): Enable WAAP Layer 7 DDoS and web application protection for CDN resources via Customer Portal Security settings with exception rules.

## Compression
- [Configure Gzip and Brotli compression](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/compression/configure-gzip-and-brotli-compression.md): Enable Gzip and Brotli edge compression on Gcore CDN with configurable MIME types; requires Origin Shielding for Brotli and Accept-Encoding header support.
- [Configure fetch compression](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/compression/configure-fetch-compression.md): Enable Fetch compressed option in CDN resource Content settings to pull pre-compressed content from origin servers supporting GZip compression.
- [Configuring HTTP status codes for CDN-hosted content](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/specify-custom-http-status-code-for-the-content-delivered-by-the-cdn.md): Configure custom HTTP status codes for CDN resources via Resource settings or Rules, with optional scheduling and redirect URLs.
- [Optimize large file delivery](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/optimize-large-file-delivery.md): Enable Large File Delivery Optimization in CDN Resource Settings to serve 10 MB+ content via chunked range requests with origin caching.
- [Rewrite: redirect requests from the CDN to the origin](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/rewrite-redirect-requests-from-the-cdn-to-the-origin.md): Configure CDN Rewrite option with nginx regex directives to redirect origin requests from one path to another using capture groups and flags.
- [WebSockets: allow permanent connections with the origin](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/websockets-allow-permanent-connections-with-the-origin.md): Enable or disable WebSocket protocol for persistent bidirectional TCP connections between CDN resources and origin servers.

## HTTP headers
- [Specify HTTP headers that CDN adds to requests to the origin](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/http-headers/specify-http-headers-that-cdn-adds-to-requests-to-the-origin.md): Configure custom HTTP request headers that CDN appends to origin requests via Request Headers option in Customer Portal.
- [Add the Access-Control-Allow-Origin header to the browser response](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/http-headers/add-the-access-control-allow-origin-header-to-the-browser-response.md): Configure Access-Control-Allow-Origin CORS header in Gcore CDN resource settings or rules to allow cross-origin requests from specified domains or any origin.
- [Add or hide response headers](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/http-headers/add-or-hide-response-headers.md): Configure custom HTTP response headers in CDN resource settings with header name, value, status code filtering, and support for multiple values per header.
- [Configure and check the host header](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/http-headers/configure-and-check-the-host-header.md): Configure and check the Host header in CDN resources using Custom Host header or Forward Host header options to ensure correct origin requests.
- [Use the X-Forwarded-Proto header to identify the protocol used for CDN servers requests](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/http-headers/use-the-x-forwarded-proto-header-to-identify-the-protocol-used-for-cdn-servers-requests.md): Configure X-Forwarded-Proto header on CDN servers to identify client protocol (HTTP/HTTPS) for conditional redirects and content delivery logic.
- [Network limits: set the content delivery speed to end users](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/network-limits-set-the-content-delivery-speed-to-end-users.md): Configure CDN Download Speed Limit with Static mode (Connection Speed, Limit After parameters) or Dynamic mode (speed, buffer query arguments) to throttle co...

## Image optimization (paid)
- [About image stack](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/image-optimization-paid/about-image-stack.md): Optimize JPG and PNG images on-the-fly using Image Stack with quality adjustment, WebP/AVIF compression, resize, and crop operations cached in CDN.
- [Enable image stack](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/image-optimization-paid/enable-image-stack.md): Request account activation via Gcore Support, then enable Image Stack in CDN resource Rules with image quality, WebP/AVIF compression, and resize/crop parame...

## Image stack tools
- [Сonfigure image compression to WebP and AVIF](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/image-optimization-paid/image-stack-tools/configure-image-compression-to-webp-and-avif.md): Enable automatic WebP and AVIF compression for JPG and PNG images via CDN Image Stack rules or query string parameters.
- [Change image quality](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/image-optimization-paid/image-stack-tools/change-image-quality.md): Reduce image quality via Image Stack option using portal settings or query string parameters with preset or custom quality values.
- [Crop images](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/image-optimization-paid/image-stack-tools/crop-images.md): Configure CDN image cropping with query string parameters to automatically crop JPG/PNG images before delivery using aspect ratio, dimensions, or gravity set...
- [Reduce image size](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/image-optimization-paid/image-stack-tools/reduce-image-size.md): Configure CDN image resize using query parameters to reduce JPG/PNG dimensions via compression or cropping before delivery.
- [Possible image stack errors](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/image-optimization-paid/possible-image-stack-errors.md): Diagnose Image Stack processing failures using HTTP status codes, response headers (X-Img-Server, X-IMG-Saved-Bytes, X-IMG-Processing-Time), and developer to...
- [Query String Forwarding: forward parameters to sub-requests](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/query-string-forwarding.md): Forward query string parameters from HLS/MPEG-DASH master manifests to nested rendition manifests and segments via CDN resource configuration or API.
- [Determine in which regions the content will be delivered with Geobalancer for paid tariffs](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/determine-in-which-regions-the-content-will-be-delivered-with-geo-balancer-for-paid-tariffs.md): Configure Geobalancer to restrict content delivery to specific regions (North America, Latin America, Asia, Middle East, Australia, Africa, Europe) and set d...

## Rules for particular files
- [Create a rule manually or from a template to configure settings for particular files](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/rules-for-particular-files/create-a-rule-manually-or-from-a-template-to-configure-settings-for-particular-files.md): Create CDN rules for specific file paths with custom caching, origin protocol, and delivery options using manual creation or predefined templates.
- [Create a CDN resource rule template manually or use a system one](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/rules-for-particular-files/create-a-cdn-resource-rule-template-manually-or-use-a-system-one.md): Create custom CDN resource rule templates with file paths, origin pull protocols, and caching options, or apply pre-built System templates for video streamin...
- [Avoid indexing of your CDN resource by web crawlers](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/cdn-resource-options/rules-for-particular-files/set-robots-txt-to-avoid-adding-to-index-by-web-crawlers.md): Prevent web crawler indexing of CDN custom domains using Status code rules to avoid duplicate content in search results and SEO degradation.
- [Clear CDN resource cache by URL, pattern, or all and check purge history](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/clear-cdn-resource-cache-by-url-pattern-or-all.md): Purge CDN resource cache by URL, pattern, or all using API or Gcore Customer Portal; check purge history and request limits.

## Grafana
- [View CDN statistics in Grafana](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/grafana/view-cdn-statistics-in-grafana.md): Display CDN metrics including traffic, cache hit ratio, bandwidth, and HTTP response codes in Grafana using the cdn-stats plugin with grouping by resource, r...
- [How the CDN service and its additional options are billed](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/how-the-cdn-service-and-its-additional-options-are-billed.md): Calculate CDN service billing based on monthly traffic limits, overage charges, and request limits with per-unit pricing for exceeded thresholds.

## IP configuration options
- [Bring Your Own IP](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/ip-configuration-options/byoip.md): Announce custom IP subnets and ASNs from Gcore infrastructure or your own autonomous system for IP address management control.
- [Dedicated IP](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/ip-configuration-options/dedicated-ip.md): Provision dedicated IP addresses or subnets for isolated content delivery and enhanced security isolation from shared IP networks.
- [Load content to the CDN before users request it](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/load-the-content-to-cdn-before-users-request-it.md): Use Prefetch to preload files (200 MB–5 GB) into CDN cache before user requests via CDN Resource paths.

## Logs
- [Logs uploader](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/logs/logs-uploader.md): Export CDN resource logs to Object Storage, S3, FTP, SFTP, or HTTP endpoints; configure policies, targets, and delivery schedules via Logs uploader.
- [Log viewer: view and download CDN resource logs](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/logs/log-viewer-view-and-download-cdn-resource-logs.md): View and download CDN resource logs with filters for time, IP, HTTP method, status code, data center, and cache status; export up to 10,000 entries per 24-ho...

## SSL certificates
- [Add an SSL certificate to deliver content over HTTPS](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/ssl-certificates/add-an-ssl-certificate-to-deliver-content-over-https.md): Attach SSL certificates to CDN resources for HTTPS delivery using custom domains with self-managed or Let's Encrypt certificates.
- [Configure your own SSL certificate](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/ssl-certificates/configure-your-own-ssl-certificate.md): Upload custom SSL certificates in PEM format with certificate chain and private key to Gcore CDN, then attach to CDN resources for HTTPS delivery on custom d...
- [Configure Let's Encrypt certificate](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/ssl-certificates/configure-lets-encrypt-certificate.md): Enable HTTPS with free Let's Encrypt certificates on CDN resources using HTTP-01 or DNS-01 challenge validation methods.

## Terraform
- [Install Terraform and integrate it with Gcore CDN](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/terraform/install-and-configure-terraform.md): Install Terraform, configure Gcore provider with API token, and initialize Terraform modules for CDN resource management.
- [Create and manage CDN resources with Terraform](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/terraform/manage-a-cdn-resource-with-terraform.md): Configure Gcore CDN resources using Terraform provider with origin groups, active/backup origins, and upstream failover options via main.tf.
- [Copy CDN resources via Terraform](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/terraform/copy-cdn-resources-via-terraform.md): Duplicate CDN resources across environments using Terraform variables, workspaces, and .tfvars files with API token authentication.
- [Create a CDN rule via Terraform](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/terraform/create-cdn-rule-via-terraform.md): Add CDN rules to Terraform-managed resources using gcore_cdn_resource with rule blocks, path patterns, and optional compression settings.
- [Add an SSL certificate to a CDN resource via Terraform](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/terraform/add-ssl-certificate-to-cdn-via-terraform.md): Configure SSL certificate for CDN resource in Terraform using main.tf with certificate name, public key, and private key parameters.
- [Set up version control and roll back to previous configurations](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/terraform/version-control-and-rollback-via-terraform.md): Integrate Terraform and Git for CDN configuration versioning, rollback procedures, and infrastructure change management with commit history and tagged releases.
- [manage-cdn-via-mcp-server](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/manage-cdn-via-mcp-server.md)

## Troubleshooting
- [Common Setup Issues](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/troubleshooting/common-setup-issues.md): Diagnose and resolve CDN setup issues including 503 errors, redirect loops, MIME type problems, and SSL certificate validation failures.
- [Resolve errors by status code for GCore CDN](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/troubleshooting/gcore-error-status-codes.md): Diagnose CDN delivery errors using status codes 403 and 404 with Log viewer, identify misconfiguration causes (disabled resources, access restrictions, origi...
- [Traceparent header for troubleshooting](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/troubleshooting/traceparent-header-for-troubleshooting.md): Implement W3C traceparent HTTP header for distributed tracing across Gcore Edge Network with trace-id and parent-id fields in requests and raw logs.
- [Content is unavailable after a CDN resource creation: how to solve the issue](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/troubleshooting/content-is-unavailable-after-a-cdn-resource-creation-how-to-solve-the-issue.md): Diagnose and resolve CDN content delivery failures by verifying origin HTTP responses, validating CNAME DNS configuration, and checking Host Header settings.
- [Cache hit ratio is low: How to solve the issue](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/troubleshooting/cache-percentage-is-low-how-to-solve-the-issue.md): Diagnose and resolve low cache hit ratio issues using Statistics metrics, response headers, and cache-control directives in Gcore CDN.
- [Content delivery speed is low: how to solve the issue](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/troubleshooting/content-delivery-speed-is-low-how-to-solve-the-issue.md): Diagnose and resolve low CDN content delivery speed by comparing origin and CDN performance, checking cache hit ratio, enabling large file optimization, and ...
- [An SSL certificate isn't working: how to solve the issue](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/troubleshooting/an-ssl-certificate-isn-t-working-how-to-solve-to-issue.md): Troubleshoot SSL certificate attachment failures by verifying certificate chain formatting and Let's Encrypt issuance by checking ACME challenge path rules a...
- [3xx error: how to solve CDN issues with redirect](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/troubleshooting/3xx-error-how-to-solve-cdn-issues-with-redirect.md): Resolve CDN 3xx redirect errors by configuring origin pull protocol, Host Header, and origin domain settings to match redirect behavior.
- [4xx error: how to solve CDN issues with the limited access and content non-existence](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/troubleshooting/4xx-error-how-to-solve-cdn-issues-with-the-limited-access-and-content-non-existence.md): Diagnose and resolve 4xx CDN errors by validating origin accessibility, allowlisting CDN edge IPs, configuring Origin Pull Protocol, and verifying Host Heade...
- [5xx error: how to solve server issues](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/troubleshooting/5xx-error-how-to-solve-server-issues.md): Diagnose and resolve HTTP 5xx errors on CDN edge servers by checking incidents, origin server availability, IP whitelisting, origin pull protocol, and Host H...

## Video CDN
- [Video CDN Overview](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/video-cdn/video-cdn-overview.md): Deliver live streams and video-on-demand content globally using Gcore CDN without transcoding
- [HTTP-FLV Live and VOD Delivery](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/video-cdn/flv-streaming.md): Technical guide for implementing FLV Live streaming and VOD delivery on Gcore CDN with configuration, authentication, failover, and observability
- [HLS Live and VOD Streaming via CDN](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/video-cdn/hls-streaming.md): Deliver HLS live streams and video-on-demand content through Gcore CDN for global scalability and performance
- [Improve video delivery speed for low-traffic accounts](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/video-cdn/improve-video-delivery-speed.md): Understand why CDN speed depends on traffic volume and learn how to reduce latency for new or small-traffic video accounts
- [Low-Latency MPEG-DASH Streaming](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/video-cdn/low-latency-mpeg-dash.md): Deliver ultra-low latency MPEG-DASH live streams with 2-4 second glass-to-glass latency using Gcore CDN
- [Secure Token for Video CDN](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/video-cdn/secure-token.md): Protect HLS and MPEG-DASH video delivery with CDN secure tokens and query string forwarding
- [Auto-refresh CDN secure tokens](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/video-cdn/secure-token-auto-refresh.md): Configure CDN Secure Token auto-refresh with 60-second expiry and query string parameters or path-based tokens to prevent URL sharing during live streams and...
- [View statistics of CDN resources](https://gcore.com/docs/cdn/view-statistics-of-a-cdn-resource.md): Access CDN resource statistics including traffic, bandwidth, response codes, cache hit ratio, and advanced analytics with filtering by countries, directories...
